question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Short answer — what likely broke - The symptoms you describe strongly match three documented incidents where an organizational change (AD/Azure AD group changes / provisioning) caused: (A) users losing access to Finance reports and creators showing as “unknown” (reports “cannot be found”), (B) Sales users being unable to place external calls because their Voice Routing Policy was changed by the new group membership, and (C) Finance users’ default printer being set incorrectly because GPO/printer assignments are location‑based and depend on AD group membership. Each of those symptom clusters appears in our ticket history after group/organizational changes

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - Sales calling failures: affected users were recently moved into a new AD/Azure AD group and an automated provisioning rule assigned them the wrong Voice Routing Policy (“Global”) which lacked PSTN usage, so external calls failed; assigning the correct routing policy (“DR‑Standard”) restored calling in tests and the provisioning logic was later corrected
context: SOURCE 9: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External number cannot be dialed Ticket number: TI-1609823 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 21 Owner: Employee 39 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 03.09.25 09:08 Closure Closure date: 12.09.25 15:40 Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing configuration adjusted according to Teams Voice Architecture. Dialing external numbers possible again. Activities 03.09.25 08:55 – Email from Contact Person 21 Good morning Some employees have been unable to dial external phone numbers since today. Internal Teams calls function normally. External calls are terminated immediately; in some cases the message “Call failed” appears. Could you please check this? Kind regards 03.09.25 09:08 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 39. 03.09.25 09:40 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Are all users affected or only specific ones? Does it affect all external numbers or only certain ones? Were there any recent changes to licenses or user roles? Thank you for your feedback. 03.09.25 10:25 – Customer response It affects about 6 users, all from the Sales team. Other teams can call normally. It affects all external numbers. We are not aware of any license changes. 03.09.25 11:15 – Internal note Test performed with affected user. Internal call → works. External call → immediate termination after dialing. Teams briefly shows “Connecting”, then “Call ended”. 04.09.25 08:50 – Email to customer Good morning We are currently reviewing the voice routing and license configuration. Can you confirm whether these users were recently moved to another team or security group? Kind regards 04.09.25 09:30 – Customer response Yes, the affected users were organizationally reassigned to the new department “Enterprise Sales” last week. New Azure AD groups were created for this purpose. 04.09.25 11:40 – Teams Admin Center analysis Users have: • Teams license • Calling Plan not active • Direct Routing configured Observation: Voice Routing Policy = “Global” Not “DR-Sales” Comparison with working user from another team shows: Voice Routing Policy = “DR-Standard” 05.09.25 09:05 – Internal review Get-CsOnlineVoiceRoutingPolicy shows that policy “Global” does not reference any PSTN Usage. Policy “DR-Standard” includes PSTN Usage “External-Route”. 05.09.25 11:20 – Email to customer Good day We have identified that the affected users are currently assigned a different Voice Routing Policy than the other employees. We are reviewing why this assignment was changed. Kind regards 08.09.25 – Internal analysis New Azure AD group “Enterprise-Sales” was created. Automated provisioning script assigns policy “Global” by default when users become members of this group. The internal document “Blueprint – Teams Voice Routing & Policy Assignment – Version 1.3” states that users with Direct Routing must be assigned a dedicated routing policy. 09.09.25 – Test phase Temporary assignment of policy “DR-Standard” to affected user. Test call to mobile phone performed. Call successful. Reverted to “Global” → issue reproducible. 10.09.25 – Coordination with Identity team Provisioning script was modified two weeks ago. New group structure was not reflected in the routing blueprint. 11.09.25 – Adjustment Policy assignment corrected according to Voice Routing architecture. Provisioning logic adjusted. 12.09.25 – Customer feedback Good day All affected users can dial external numbers again. Longer calls also function normally. Thank you for your support. 12.09.25 15:40 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 21 The root cause was an incorrect Voice Routing Policy assignment following an organizational restructuring. The routing configuration was adjusted according to the internal Teams Voice Architecture. External calls are now possible again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: . - Wrong/default printer mapping: after a departmental AD group change the Finance group had inconsistent memberships and became (indirectly) member of the Zurich location group, so the Zurich printer was applied via GPO as default for Basel users; removing the incorrect group membership and forcing a gpupdate fixed the default printer behavior
context: SOURCE 2: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF60: Incorrect printer automatically set as default Ticket number: TI-1623017 Company: Customer Company 60 AG Contact person: Contact Person 18 Owner: Employee 23 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 18.11.25 08:49 Closure Closure date: 24.11.25 15:11 Closure text: Printer assignment and default configuration corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Behavior stable. Activities 18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18 Good morning Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location. They must manually select the correct printer before each print job. The issue seems to have started last week. Kind regards 18.11.25 08:49 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23. 18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis Remote connection established with an affected user. Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”. Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list. Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set. 18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer Good day Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department? Kind regards 18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week. The old group was deactivated. 19.11.25 09:10 – GPO assignment analysis Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”. In the section Printer Provisioning it is defined that: • Printer assignments are location-based via AD groups • Default printers per location are set via GPO • No parallel groups with identical function may exist Review shows that the new Finance group is additionally a member of a higher-level Zurich location group. As a result, the Zurich printer is also mapped via GPO and set as default. 19.11.25 11:30 – Test Temporary removal of the incorrect group membership. gpupdate /force executed. User logged in again. Default printer now correctly remains “MFP-Basel-01”. 20.11.25 – Coordination with customer Confirmation that the group structure had not been fully cleaned up during the reorganization. Correction performed according to blueprint specification. 24.11.25 14:50 – Customer feedback Multiple users tested. Default printer remains correct. No further automatic switching. 24.11.25 15:11 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 18 The root cause was an inconsistent group membership following an organizational adjustment. Printer assignment was corrected according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The correct location printer now remains permanently set as default. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company

context: SOURCE 9: ﻿ Ticket summary: Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External number cannot be dialed Ticket number: TI-1609823 Company: Customer Company 52 AG Contact person: Contact Person 21 Owner: Employee 39 Origin: Email Type: Incident Created on: 03.09.25 09:08 Closure Closure date: 12.09.25 15:40 Closure text: Analysis completed. Routing configuration adjusted according to Teams Voice Architecture. Dialing external numbers possible again. Activities 03.09.25 08:55 – Email from Contact Person 21 Good morning Some employees have been unable to dial external phone numbers since today. Internal Teams calls function normally. External calls are terminated immediately; in some cases the message “Call failed” appears. Could you please check this? Kind regards 03.09.25 09:08 Ticket created and assigned to Employee 39. 03.09.25 09:40 – Helpdesk inquiry Good day Are all users affected or only specific ones? Does it affect all external numbers or only certain ones? Were there any recent changes to licenses or user roles? Thank you for your feedback. 03.09.25 10:25 – Customer response It affects about 6 users, all from the Sales team. Other teams can call normally. It affects all external numbers. We are not aware of any license changes. 03.09.25 11:15 – Internal note Test performed with affected user. Internal call → works. External call → immediate termination after dialing. Teams briefly shows “Connecting”, then “Call ended”. 04.09.25 08:50 – Email to customer Good morning We are currently reviewing the voice routing and license configuration. Can you confirm whether these users were recently moved to another team or security group? Kind regards 04.09.25 09:30 – Customer response Yes, the affected users were organizationally reassigned to the new department “Enterprise Sales” last week. New Azure AD groups were created for this purpose. 04.09.25 11:40 – Teams Admin Center analysis Users have: • Teams license • Calling Plan not active • Direct Routing configured Observation: Voice Routing Policy = “Global” Not “DR-Sales” Comparison with working user from another team shows: Voice Routing Policy = “DR-Standard” 05.09.25 09:05 – Internal review Get-CsOnlineVoiceRoutingPolicy shows that policy “Global” does not reference any PSTN Usage. Policy “DR-Standard” includes PSTN Usage “External-Route”. 05.09.25 11:20 – Email to customer Good day We have identified that the affected users are currently assigned a different Voice Routing Policy than the other employees. We are reviewing why this assignment was changed. Kind regards 08.09.25 – Internal analysis New Azure AD group “Enterprise-Sales” was created. Automated provisioning script assigns policy “Global” by default when users become members of this group. The internal document “Blueprint – Teams Voice Routing & Policy Assignment – Version 1.3” states that users with Direct Routing must be assigned a dedicated routing policy. 09.09.25 – Test phase Temporary assignment of policy “DR-Standard” to affected user. Test call to mobile phone performed. Call successful. Reverted to “Global” → issue reproducible. 10.09.25 – Coordination with Identity team Provisioning script was modified two weeks ago. New group structure was not reflected in the routing blueprint. 11.09.25 – Adjustment Policy assignment corrected according to Voice Routing architecture. Provisioning logic adjusted. 12.09.25 – Customer feedback Good day All affected users can dial external numbers again. Longer calls also function normally. Thank you for your support. 12.09.25 15:40 – Ticket closure Dear Contact Person 21 The root cause was an incorrect Voice Routing Policy assignment following an organizational restructuring. The routing configuration was adjusted according to the internal Teams Voice Architecture. External calls are now possible again. Kind regards Consulting Sample Company
